description: Become different in a relevant respect, or make something different. The verb does not say how extensive, welcome, or lasting the difference is. The surrounding clause can supply the feature, earlier state, or result when the distinction matters.
articulatory_notes: Let unvoiced breath pass through the open glottis for 'h' into middle-height front 'e'. Put the tongue tip at the alveolar ridge for stressed 'l' and round into high-back 'u', then keep the final high-front 'i' in its own syllable without a glide.
sound_symbolism: Breath gathers around the lateral, then opens a second vowel after stressed 'lu'. The shifting mouth shape recalls difference without suggesting a fixed direction.
usage_notes: The changing thing may be the subject, or an agent may take the changed thing as an object. 'lue' (from) and 'muo' (into) can state endpoints. 'moluki' (transform) suits extensive reorganisation; 'kelu' (become) centres the resulting predicate.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Impermanence can be stated at the scale actually observed: a belief, season, or material changes without the sentence claiming that everything has changed.

semantic_domains.cognition: revision of a belief, interpretation, or mental state
semantic_domains.physical: an observable difference in material or condition
semantic_domains.temporal: difference between an earlier and a later state
